Description.Name: Ashes Settled
Description.Details: The Argo continues its orbit of Butzfleth Majoris in silence. The mech bay still smells like burned coolant and scorched armor plating. Yang is the first to talk about it, leaning against a bulkhead in the mess hall while the rest of the crew picks at rations. "Prisoners. He was shipping prisoners to build bunkers. People doing forced labor in some frozen hole because a warden decided they were worth more as bodies than as inmates." He shakes his head. "I've worked jobs where the line between contractor and conscript got thin. But this wasn't thin. This was gone." Darius sets down his mug. "The kill was clean. That's not what's bothering me. What's bothering me is whether it actually changes anything. Rooke was the middleman. The Tribe needed labor, he supplied it. We cut him out, they find someone else. Another warden, another frontier governor with a flexible conscience. The demand doesn't go away just because we removed one supplier." Before anyone can respond, Sumire flags an incoming transmission on the bridge console. Another [[DM.Factions[faction_GhostBearWatch],Ghost Bear]] encrypted signal. She reads it aloud. "Rooke is eliminated. Labor flow to the [[DM.Factions[faction_MinnesotaTribe],Tribe]] has ceased. Next target: Cygnus system. Expect hostile activity to be increased. Full mission briefing upon arrival." Sumire stares at the screen for a moment. "Labor flow has ceased. That's how she describes it. Not prisoners freed, not people rescued. Labor flow. Like she turned off a supply line for raw materials." Nobody argues, nobody disagrees.

Description.Name: Prepare to leave for Cygnus
Description.Details: Back at the bridge, you tap the console, pulling up the jump sequence, then pause. "Farah, what are we walking into with Cygnus III?" Farah looks over and taps her datapad, then slides it your way. "Terran-class planet, rich biomes. Everything from polar tundra and alpine slopes to badlands, jungles, and lowland forests. Pretty on the surface. Deadly once the weather rolls in." She swipes to another screen. "The atmosphere's unstable. Constant electrical storms, high static charge, interference so strong it bricks half your sensors. Star League used to run weather stations here. Most are ruins now, fried by the storms or torn down by time." She shows a strange violet flower blooming from a bed of frost. "This is the tundra witch. Native to the cold zones. Its pollen's psychoactive. Triggers hallucinations in most species. Draws megafauna like a magnet. Massive grazers that'll stampede over anything that moves." She gives a short breath. "Smugglers still come in, trying to harvest the pollen for narcotics. Most vanish. Either taken by the storms, or trampled into mulch." Farah meets your eyes. "If the Tribe's working here, it's not random. They're hiding something… and they're hoping the planet keeps it hidden." [A new flashpoint has been created in the Cygnus system to continue the campaign]
